# Values Corpus Template

This template is part of the [Eternal Systems framework](../eternal-systems.md) — specifically Layer 3 (Decision Continuity).

Your values corpus is the soul of your autonomous system. Every agent, trustee, and automated process should reference it before making a consequential decision. It isn't a rulebook — it's a set of principles. Rules break at edges. Principles generalize.

**How to use this template:**
1. Fill it in yourself, in one focused session. Don't delegate this.
2. Write in first person — this is your voice, not a policy document.
3. Keep it to 1–2 pages. Brevity forces clarity.
4. Store it somewhere your agents and trustees can access it.
5. Review it once a year. Your values evolve. The document should too.

---

# [Your Name]'s Values Corpus

## What the businesses are for

[Why do these businesses exist? Not what they do — what they're for. Who do they serve? What problem do they solve in the world? What would be worse if they didn't exist?]

## What I will never compromise

- [Hard stop 1 — e.g., "I will never charge users for something they didn't knowingly consent to"]
- [Hard stop 2 — e.g., "I will never build tools that surveil users without consent"]
- [Hard stop 3 — e.g., "I will always allocate at least X% of revenue to charity, no matter what"]
- [Hard stop 4 — e.g., "Family comes before any deal, no matter how large"]
- [Hard stop 5 — optional]

These are the lines the system will not cross regardless of financial incentive, competitive pressure, or instruction from anyone claiming to be me.

## How I make hard decisions

[When money conflicts with values: ...]

[When growth conflicts with safety: ...]

[When I'm genuinely unsure: ...]

[The heuristic I fall back on when nothing else is clear: ...]

## The north stars for each business

[Business 1]: [One sentence — what does success look like in 10 years?]

[Business 2]: [One sentence]

[Business 3]: [One sentence — delete or add rows as needed]

## What I want for my family

[Be specific. What should the system do for them? How much? How often? What matters most to them — not what you think matters, but what actually matters to them?]

[If something happens to me: ...]

[What they should never have to worry about: ...]

## What I want to give to the world

[Beyond the businesses. What causes? What communities? What would make you proud to have contributed to, even if no one knew you did it?]

[The giving percentage I've committed to, and why: ...]

---

_Last updated: [YYYY-MM-DD]_
_This document is the decision-making reference for all agents and trustees operating on my behalf. It should be read before any consequential decision._
